skill.name: azure-search-documents-py
skill.description: Guidance for building Python applications with Azure AI Search, including indexing, vector search, hybrid search, semantic ranking, facets, filters, and skillsets.
skill.summary: Build Azure AI Search workflows in Python with practical SDK examples.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, script, network, environment, and heuristic findings, but review found a single Markdown documentation file with Python and shell examples. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable helper scripts, or malicious intent was found. Publishable with a low-risk note because examples include API key and environment variable usage.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Executable Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings occur inside Markdown code fences and tables that document installation, imports, SDK calls, and examples. They are not standalone scripts executed by the skill, so the command execution risk is a false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced Markdown examples, including pip install and Python SDK usage. No executable script file or automatic command runner exists in the package.

low_findings.title: Python Imports Misclassified as Dynamic Import Expressions
low_findings.description: The script findings point to normal Python import blocks in SDK examples. They do not use runtime import loading, eval, exec, or user-controlled module names.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows static import statements for Azure SDK and Azure exception classes. These examples are documentation and contain no dynamic code execution path.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Service URL and API Key Examples
low_findings.description: The skill documents Azure Search endpoint and API key environment variables. This is legitimate SDK setup guidance, but users should avoid committing real secrets and should prefer Entra ID for production.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines clearly reference environment variables and API key authentication. The values are placeholders and the skill recommends DefaultAzureCredential, so this is a low-risk documentation concern.

low_findings.title: Critical Heuristic Not Confirmed by Semantic Review
low_findings.description: The analyzer combined code examples, Azure endpoint placeholders, and credential placeholders into a dangerous combination alert. Review found no outbound exfiltration endpoint, no hidden script, and no prompt-injection content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The package contains only SKILL.md, and the suspicious elements are documented Azure setup patterns. No evidence found of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or instructions to bypass security review.

content.user_title: Build Azure AI Search Apps in Python
content.value_statement: Python teams often need exact SDK patterns for Azure AI Search without searching across many docs. This skill provides focused examples for indexes, documents, vector queries, hybrid search, semantic ranking, and skillsets.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install and configure the azure-search-documents Python package.
Explains SearchClient, SearchIndexClient, and SearchIndexerClient usage.
Provides examples for vector, keyword, hybrid, and semantic search patterns.
Documents index creation with vector fields, HNSW configuration, and semantic settings.
Covers document upload, batch indexing, filters, facets, autocomplete, and suggest APIs.
Includes guidance for skillsets, indexers, data sources, and async client usage.
limitations: Does not include executable helper scripts in this package.
Does not provision Azure resources or validate service configuration.
Uses examples that must be adapted to each index schema and embedding model.
References extra files that are not present in this packaged skill.

use_cases.title: Create a Search Index
use_cases.description: Design a Python index definition with searchable fields, vector fields, and semantic configuration.

use_cases.title: Add Hybrid Retrieval
use_cases.description: Combine keyword search, vector similarity, filters, and semantic ranking for retrieval applications.

use_cases.title: Build Indexing Pipelines
use_cases.description: Plan document upload, buffered indexing, indexers, data sources, and enrichment skillsets.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Search Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the Python setup for Azure AI Search using SearchClient and DefaultAzureCredential.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Vector Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define an Azure AI Search index with text fields, a vector field, and HNSW configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Hybrid Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Python search flow that combines keyword text, vector queries, filters, and semantic ranking.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Enriched Indexer
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Azure AI Search indexing pipeline with a blob data source, skillset, indexer, and retry-aware document uploads.

output_examples.input: I need a quick setup for Azure AI Search in Python.
output_examples.output: A concise setup path covering package installation, environment variables, credential choice, and SearchClient initialization.

output_examples.input: I want semantic and vector retrieval for product documents.
output_examples.output: A guided plan for vector fields, embedding dimensions, HNSW profiles, semantic configuration, and hybrid query structure.

output_examples.input: I need to index documents from Azure Blob Storage.
output_examples.output: A pipeline outline for data source connection, skillset creation, indexer configuration, and operational best practices.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production authentication when possible.
Match vector dimensions to the embedding model used by the application.
Batch document uploads and use buffered sender patterns for larger indexing jobs.
anti_patterns: Do not commit real Azure Search API keys or connection strings.
Do not mix embedding models with indexes that expect different vector dimensions.
Do not rely only on vector search when keyword filters or semantic ranking are required.

faq.question: Does this skill run Azure commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance skill with Python and shell examples.

faq.question: Which Python package does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on the azure-search-documents package and related Azure identity patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with vector search?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes vector field setup, vector queries, HNSW configuration, and hybrid retrieval examples.

faq.question: Does it recommend API keys?
faq.answer: It documents API key usage, but also marks DefaultAzureCredential as the preferred production approach.

faq.question: Can it create Azure resources for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance that users must run in their own Azure environment.

faq.question: Is it useful in Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples can guide code generation and review in Claude, Claude Code, and Codex workflows.
